title: Shared Sources, Tenant Subscriptions, State Machines, and Races · Inference Brief: Feed Ts Source Authorization
summary: Equivalent URLs create duplicate pollers, unfollow removes shared entries, source state is a boolean, IDs imply authorization, and check-then-insert races remain. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed ts source authorization increment in idempotent shared-source subscription service. Learners progress through…

theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 1907 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile shared sources, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle transitions, authorization, and registration races; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
